story.iWant: to invoke editing on a specific slide via `/edit [n]`
story.soThat: I can refine any slide in my deck or single slide

tasks.task: Create Edit Workflow Structure 1.1: Create `.slide-builder/workflows/edit/` directory 1.2: Create `workflow.yaml` with name, description, instructions path 1.3: Create `instructions.md` with step-by-step workflow execution logic

Implement Edit Command Router 2.1: Parse `/edit` command arguments (optional slide number) 2.2: Read `status.yaml` to determine current mode (single vs deck) 2.3: In single mode: target `.slide-builder/single/slide.html` 2.4: In deck mode with number: target `.slide-builder/deck/slides/slide-{n}.html` 2.5: In deck mode without number: prompt user for slide number or show list 2.6: Validate target slide exists before proceeding 2.7: Return error with valid range if invalid slide number provided 2.8: Return error directing to `/build-one` if no slides exist

Implement Slide Loader 3.1: Load slide HTML content from target path 3.2: Load corresponding `slide-state.json` (or create empty if missing) 3.3: Parse HTML to extract all data-field values (current structure) 3.4: Extract current layout type from HTML structure/class names 3.5: Count existing edits from state file 3.6: Display slide info summary to user

Implement Edit Instruction Prompt 4.1: After slide loaded, prompt user: "Describe the layout change you want" 4.2: Capture user's natural language edit instruction 4.3: Confirm understanding: "I'll: [interpretation of instruction]" 4.4: Show: "Your text edits ([count]) will be preserved" 4.5: Ask: "Proceed? (y/n)" - if no, return to prompt

Register /edit Slash Command 5.1: Add edit command to Claude Code skills system 5.2: Map `/sb:edit` to edit workflow 5.3: Support optional argument for slide number 5.4: Update help/usage documentation

Testing and Edge Cases 6.1: Test single mode edit invocation 6.2: Test deck mode with valid slide number 6.3: Test deck mode with invalid slide number 6.4: Test with no slides existing 6.5: Test deck mode without number (prompt behavior) 6.6: Run Frontend Test Gate checklist

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given slides exist (single mode or deck mode), when the user runs `/edit` (no number, single mode), then the single slide is loaded for editing
Given slides exist in deck mode, when the user runs `/edit 3`, then slide 3 is loaded for editing
Given an invalid slide number is provided, when `/edit 99` is run but only 5 slides exist, then error message shows valid range (1-5)
When a slide is loaded for editing, then the current slide content summary is displayed (layout type, field count, existing edit count)
When a slide is loaded for editing, then the user is prompted for edit instructions
Given no slides exist, when `/edit` is run, then error message directs user to run `/build-one` first

docs.doc: Defines acceptance criteria, Edit Command Router module, Slide Loader module, command parsing specification, and complete /edit workflow phases.
Describes mode detection from status.yaml - single vs deck mode. In single mode targets single/slide.html, in deck mode targets deck/slides/slide-{n}.html.
User story definition: invoke editing on specific slide via /edit [n] to refine any slide in deck or single slide.
FR37: Users can edit any slide via `/edit [slide-number]`. FR38-41 cover layout changes and edit preservation.

Establishes localStorage as primary persistence. slide-state.json schema: { slide, edits: [{selector, content}], lastModified }. Auto-save script pattern with [Slide Auto-Save] console logging prefix.

file.reason: Existing edit workflow skeleton - needs enhancement for AC4.1.1-4.1.6
file.note: Already defines installed_path, instructions path, status_file, theme_file variables. Needs: mode detection, slide number argument parsing, validation logic.

file.reason: Existing basic edit instructions - needs enhancement for command routing and slide loading
file.note: Has basic structure for mode detection and slide loading. Needs: detailed error messages with valid range, slide info display, edit count from state file.

file.reason: Contains mode (single/deck), total_slides, built_count for validation
file.note: Current mode: single. Read this first to determine which slide path to target.

file.reason: Single mode slide plan structure reference
file.note: Contains intent, suggested_template, key_points, visual_guidance, tone fields.

file.reason: Reference for contenteditable and data-field patterns used in slide HTML
file.note: All text elements have contenteditable="true" and unique data-field attributes. Auto-save script uses localStorage key 'slide-edits'.

file.reason: Example slide HTML structure for parsing layout type and fields
file.note: Shows .slide container, CSS structure, element hierarchy. Use as reference for extracting layout type.

package.name: googleapis
package.purpose: Google Slides API (Epic 7, not this story)

dependencies.note: No npm dependencies required for Story 4.1 - uses Claude Code built-in capabilities only.

constraints.constraint: Workflow follows BMAD pattern: workflow.yaml contains config, instructions.md contains execution logic with XML step structure
Mode detection must read status.yaml to determine single vs deck mode before targeting slide path
In single mode: target .slide-builder/single/slide.html; In deck mode: target .slide-builder/deck/slides/slide-{n}.html
Validate slide exists before proceeding; return helpful error with valid range if not found
Display current slide info for user context: layout type, field count, existing edit count
Read slide-state.json for edit count; schema is { slide, edits: [{selector, content}], lastModified }
Users invoke editing via /edit [slide-number] - number is optional in single mode

interface.name: status.yaml
interface.signature: mode: single | deck current_slide: number total_slides: number built_count: number

interface.name: slide-state.json
/sb:edit command
interface.note: Optional slide number argument. If omitted in single mode, edits single/slide.html. If omitted in deck mode, prompts for slide selection.

tests.standards: Per Architecture test strategy, tests should be manual verification following the Frontend Test Gate pattern. Each AC maps to a specific test scenario. Tests cover: mode detection (single vs deck from status.yaml), path targeting (correct slide.html path constructed), validation (invalid slide numbers return error with range), edge cases (no slides returns error directing to /build-one), and slide info display (layout type, fields, edit count correctly extracted).

locations.location: Frontend Test Gate in story file (4-1-TG1)
Manual CLI testing via /sb:edit command invocations

ideas.idea: In single mode (status.yaml mode: single), run /sb:edit without arguments. Verify system loads .slide-builder/single/slide.html and displays slide info.
Set up deck mode with 5 slides. Run /sb:edit 3. Verify system loads .slide-builder/deck/slides/slide-3.html.
In deck mode with 5 slides, run /sb:edit 99. Verify error message: "Invalid slide number. Valid range: 1-5".
After slide loads, verify CLI output shows: layout type (e.g., "Title slide"), field count (e.g., "3 text fields"), edit count (e.g., "2 saved edits").
After slide info displayed, verify user receives prompt: "Describe the layout change you want".
Delete all slide.html files. Run /sb:edit. Verify error: "No slide to edit. Run /build-one first."
In deck mode, run /sb:edit without slide number. Verify system prompts "Which slide? (1-N)" where N is total_slides.
